page: Microsoft Azure E8ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. The architecture is arm64, with a cache configuration of 64 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 128 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong memory capabilities, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Memory bandwidth tests show 909.36 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 164.94 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, alongside a write speed of 71.92 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression performs strongly in the top 25% at 33.14 GB/sec. Most general compute benchmarks, including Geekbench single-core (1668.0) and multi-core (9365.0) scores, fall within the average range. However, encryption performance is weak, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring 1494.02, placing it in the bottom 25%, which is a notable tradeoff for cryptographic workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8ps_v6 offers a high-density memory profile relative to its 8-vCPU compute allocation, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of higher vCPU tiers. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers such as Redis (achieving 16.11 million operations per second in SET benchmarks), and memory-intensive web serving. It is less suitable for heavy cryptographic tasks or complex LLM inference, where its medium-model prompt processing speed falls into the bottom 10%.
description: Microsoft Azure E8ps_v6 is an arm64 virtual machine from the Epsv6 family, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor and 64.0 GB of RAM. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal top-tier memory performance, with a PassMark memory score in the top 10% and strong single-threaded gzip compression. However, cryptographic performance is a notable tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25% on encryption tests. Given its high memory density and average compute capabilities, the E8ps_v6 is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and web serving, while being less optimal for heavy cryptographic or large-scale AI inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8ps_v6 is an arm64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Neoverse-N2 vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory performance, making it suitable for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8ps_v6 is an arm64 virtual machine with 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
tagline: An ARM-based virtual machine featuring high memory density and top-tier memory performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated arm64 vCPUs powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor
64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio
Top-tier memory performance with a PassMark memory score in the top 10%
No local storage or GPU accelerators included, but Premium Storage capable
Best suited for memory-intensive database operations, caching, and web serving
